_topics.summary: Star-shaped radius field r(theta,phi). One radius per direction. Localized bumps as primary deformation primitive. Global harmonics deprioritized.
Leaning: whole bump direction tilts as rigid offset d'=Rd. Low cost, useful for push/pull.
Gradual bending: rotation varies progressively along bump extent via slerp(I, R_max, t). Curvature via transported frames, not joints.
Collision operates on transformed evaluation points after local frame transport. Bending creates new effective collision geometry. Must preserve deformation gradients for sensory use.
Creature owns transform/orientation/dynamics. Blob field owns deformation params, bump frames, collision representation. Creature orientation mandatory — appendage directions rotate with body.
Target: smooth volumetric soft bodies via GPU particles, gaussian splatting, normal-oriented billboards. Camera-facing billboards cause fiber artifacts at bumps — use normal-oriented quads instead.

Deformation is part of information-processing architecture. Collision/pressure/bending/strain provide tactile-proprioceptive data. Some protrusions serve purely sensory roles (whiskers, feelers). Overly simplified collision responses destroy sensory gradients.
Approximate volume preservation. Flattening redistributes outward. Stiffness as unified parameter. Not strict incompressibility.
Explicitly rejected: skeletal soft bodies, FEM, metaball chains, capsule chains, per-particle collision, arbitrary mesh sculpting, unrestricted topology, strict IFS purity.
